steps.title: Chaining the multipliers
steps.content: Percentage increases are chained by multiplication. The 2012-2013 increase of 12% means multiplying by $1.12$. The 2013-2014 increase of 5% means multiplying by $1.05$. Therefore, the ratio of 2014 earnings to 2012 earnings is: $$y = 1.12 \times 1.05$$

steps.title: Calculate the product
steps.content: Expand $(1 + 0.12)(1 + 0.05)$: $$1.12 \times 1.05 = 1 + 0.12 + 0.05 + (0.12)(0.05) = 1 + 0.17 + 0.0060 = 1.1760$$ That matches the correct answer, so D is correct.
